steps.title: Test Lang's mechanism
steps.content: Lang's claim needs two pieces: a way for hydrogen gas to be trapped in water ice and a way for that gas to escape while the object moves through the solar system. Evidence for both would directly support the proposed cause.

steps.title: Reject weaker support
steps.content: Choice A only criticizes other models. Choice B contradicts the water-ice hydrogen mechanism and points to molecular hydrogen ice instead. Choice C merely says nongravitational effects can be hard to detect. The answer is D.
